pattern: Sorting then placing the larger half of the values into the odd slots and the smaller half into the even slots, both filled from their high end inward, guarantees a strict up-down zig-zag even when many values repeat.
hints: If you sort the numbers first, the small half and the large half are exactly the two groups you will interleave.
Putting one half on the even positions and the other half on the odd positions almost works, but equal values landing next to each other across the seam can break the strict inequality.

Fill the even indices with the smaller half and the odd indices with the larger half, and within each half walk from its largest element downward, so duplicates in the middle are pushed as far apart as possible.

edge_inputs.description: values with several repeats that must interleave to a zig-zag
two distinct elements form one rising step
many equal-ish values cluster around the median and must be split across halves
a heavy run of one value plus a few others stresses the median split
all identical except endpoints; valid only if the split keeps equals apart

stress.description: random arrays with a narrow value range so duplicates are dense and the median split is exercised
stress.note: narrow range forces many ties, the hard part of wiggle sort

stress.description: random arrays over a wide range

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def wiggleSort(self, nums):
        s = sorted(nums)
        n = len(nums)
        half = (n + 1) // 2
        small = s[:half]
        large = s[half:]
        small.reverse()
        large.reverse()
        res = [0] * n
        res[0::2] = small
        res[1::2] = large
        for i in range(n):
            nums[i] = res[i]
